CAN_MODIFY_PROJECT_IAM.description:
Can change project IAM bindings/roles. Can add an IAM binding making yourself administrator thus essentially controlling all resources in project.  Warning: This will wipe all previous IAM bindings with a new set of IAM bindings so its sa good idea to run get-iam-policy first and then set-iam-policy with the same policy bindings albeit your modified content
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Gcloud Commands: gcloud projects get-iam-policy my-project --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json like adding yourself as admin;  gcloud projects set-iam-policy my-project policy.json

CAN_MODIFY_FOLDER_IAM.description:
Can change folder IAM bindings/roles. This can grant control over all descendant projects/resources via inherited IAM. Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy <FOLDER_ID> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud resource-manager folders set-iam-policy <FOLDER_ID> policy.json

CAN_MODIFY_ORG_IAM.description:
Can change organization IAM bindings/roles. This can grant broad tenancy-wide control across folders and projects. Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy <ORG_ID> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud organizations set-iam-policy <ORG_ID> policy.json

CAN_MODIFY_SA_IAM.description:
Can change service account IAM bindings/roles (for example, adding Token Creator or actAs members). Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud iam service-accounts get-iam-policy <SA_EMAIL> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud iam service-accounts set-iam-policy <SA_EMAIL> policy.json

CAN_MODIFY_COMPUTE_INSTANCE_IAM.description:
Can change IAM policy on specific Compute Engine VM instances (who can operate/manage that VM). Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud compute instances get-iam-policy <INSTANCE_NAME> --zone <ZONE> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud compute instances set-iam-policy <INSTANCE_NAME> policy.json --zone <ZONE>

CAN_MODIFY_ClOUD_RUN_FUNCTION_IAM.description:
Can change IAM policy on Cloud Functions (for example, granting invoker/admin access). Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud functions get-iam-policy <FUNCTION_NAME> --region <REGION> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud functions set-iam-policy <FUNCTION_NAME> policy.json --region <REGION>

CAN_MODIFY_SECRET_MANAGER_SECRET_IAM.description:
Can change IAM policy on Secret Manager (for example, granting roles/owner to the caller so you can read the secret). Warning: set-iam-policy replaces the full policy, so fetch first and then re-apply with your intended changes.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_setiampolicy Commands: gcloud functions get-iam-policy <FUNCTION_NAME> --region <REGION> --format=json > policy.json; #edit policy.json; gcloud functions set-iam-policy <FUNCTION_NAME> policy.json --region <REGION>

CAN_IMPERSONATE_SA.description:
Can "act as" another Service Account thus getting that destination Service Account permissions. Can be chained across multiple service accounts. Note the "--impersonate-service-account" is a flag  supported on the gcloud base binary itself.
GCPwn Module: Review existing exploit_* modules for --implicit-del flag where you can specify  service accounts you want to chain before final action (ex. modules run exploit_generate_access_token --impliict-del) Commands: gcloud auth print-access-token --impersonate-service-account=TARGET_SA@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com,DELEGATE_SA@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com

CAN_CREATE_SA_ACCESS_TOKEN.description:
Can mint OAuth access tokens for a service account, enabling direct API actions as that identity. You can then reload these OAuth tokens back into gcpwn to begin calling APIs as that user ([2] oauth2   <credential_name> --token <access_token> [--tokeninfo])
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_generate_access_token Commands: gcloud auth print-access-token

CAN_CREATE_SA_KEY.description:
Can create long-lasting APIkeys for a service account, enabling direct API actions as that identity. You can then reload this key tokens back into gcpwn to begin calling APIs as that user (3] service-acc-key <credential_name> --service-file <service_account_json_path>)
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_service_account_keys Commands: gcloud iam service-accounts keys create <FILE_TO_SAVE_PRIVATE_KEY> --iam-account=SA_NAME@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com

CAN_READ_SECRET_DATA.description:
Can access Secret Manager secret payloads. Prioritize secrets tied to CI/CD, cloud auth, and production apps.
GCPwn Module: modules run enum_secretsmanager --download Commands: gcloud secrets versions access VERSION_ID (can be 'latest') --secret=SECRET_ID

roles/owner.description: Collapsed dangerous-path edge for owner role grants (roles/owner). This edge collapses all matched dangerous rule edges for the same grant->target path. See dangerous_rule_names and dangerous_rule_descriptions on the edge for the individual rules that were collapsed.

roles/editor.description: Collapsed dangerous-path edge for editor role grants (roles/editor). This edge collapses all matched dangerous rule edges for the same grant->target path. See dangerous_rule_names and dangerous_rule_descriptions on the edge for the individual rules that were collapsed.

RESET_COMPUTE_STARTUP_SA.description:
Rewrite startup-script metadata and reset a running compute instance so payload executes, paired with iam.serviceAccounts.actAs on target service accounts for execution-as-SA paths.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_instance_startup_script Commands: gcloud compute instances add-metadata <INSTANCE_NAME> --zone <ZONE> --metadata startup-script='<COMMAND>'; gcloud compute instances reset <INSTANCE_NAME> --zone <ZONE>

START_COMPUTE_STARTUP_SA.description:
Rewrite startup-script metadata and start a stopped/terminated compute instance so payload executes, paired with iam.serviceAccounts.actAs on target service accounts for execution-as-SA paths.
GCPwn Module: modules run exploit_instance_startup_script Commands: gcloud compute instances add-metadata <INSTANCE_NAME> --zone <ZONE> --metadata startup-script='<COMMAND>'; gcloud compute instances start <INSTANCE_NAME> --zone <ZONE>

CREATE_AND_INVOKE_CLOUDFUNCTION_AS_SA.description: Create a Cloud Function and attach a SA to retrieve the SA token.

UPDATE_AND_INVOKE_CLOUDFUNCTION_AS_SA.description: Update a Cloud Function with your own source code to retrieve the SA token of the existing SA or optinall leverage iam.serviceAccounts.actAs to attach a new SA to retrieve.

CREATE_CLOUDSCHEDULER_JOB_AS_SA.description: Create a Cloud Scheduler job and attach a target service account via iam.serviceAccounts.actAs, enabling execution under that service account identity.
